reviews.review_description: This game looked as basic as it gets, zombie scavenging game… but it becomes much more the further you go into it, do not sleep on this for the price, it’s a whole experience that tricks you into thinking it’s a bog standard zombie romp… but it isn’t.

The game play is really smooth, and the guns work well. But one issue I do have is the darkness. And the flashlight barley working even when upgraded. The night vision does make up for this, but it can be difficult to get the the night vision when you can't see. Other than that I think this is a great game with great graphics.

It’s very satisfying to shoot zombies in this game, with good body destruction and character physics. The graphics are surprisingly not bad - it feels better than in the trailer. The game is simple, but gameplay-driven and engaging. You shoot zombies while collecting resources, which is cool and fun. Yes, there are minor rough edges, but they don’t interfere with enjoying the gameplay. Overall, I was pleasantly surprised by the game’s quality and the good level of gameplay.

Good game with a lot of potential. I'd like to be able to have a second weapon and improve the visual quality of the weapons to make them more realistic. And more weapons and grenades. And more varied enemies like in Left 4 Dead, etc.

This game is a real hidden gem! I love my zombie games, and if you do too, you’ll find this one both addictive and fun. The graphics (diverse and realistic environments to walk/run through) and sound design are fantastic, some of the zombie noises are quite unintentionally funny, no bugs encountered by me yet either. But what really makes the game shine is the scavenging and progression system.

I started out by looting everything I could to boost my ammo capacity and movement speed, and to grab a few great attachments for my pistol. There are loads of weapons to unlock, I’ve opened up five so far, they all handle well yet I still keep coming back to my trusty suppressed pistol with a laser sight for those satisfying heads shots.

I’ll admit I’m a bit of a coward, so I prefer grinding special items early on before tackling the tougher missions. Thankfully, the game really lets you play your own way.  If you’re a better shot than me, you could unlock the various areas sooner. Supply drop missions unlock new weapons and ammo, but they’re definitely more stressful having to listen to the beeping of the crate to locate it before it self-destructs whilst zombie hoards are on your case!

The game is currently on sale in the UK for just £1.99, which is an absolute steal. It’s a simple premise, but it’s so well executed, and the dev is also very active on Discord, which is a great bonus if you need guidance. Highly recommended for easy stress relief!
There is a good amount of game here for the money.  Good balance between action and problem solving.  Great atmosphere and graphic, very immersing.

I give it 3 prestige stars! It is definitely a step up from the first game. The graphics are good, the levels are designed really well, the weapons are fun to use.  I wished there was an actual storyline and I do mean a long one kinda like resident evil or something but I loved what the game offered anyway. I thought you guys did a great job, from the graphics to the gameplay. 

Now...for the sound guy I have an issue with you sir or madam. Idk what it is with all these VR games but blasting the music at 100 where I cant hear zombies, guns, I couldn't even hear the guy at the end of the escape level because the music was so loud and before you suggest I turn it off I did but it would come back on so when I'd load into the game I'd have to switch the sound from off to on and back off so it registered. I live game sound tracks as much as the next guy but not like this. Please fix the music volume. Thank you

developer_response.body: Thanks for leaving a review! You can actually adjust the music volume in the options which are located in the hideout.

reviews.review_description: Loving the game so far, only thing id add is a seated mode, I hate standing to play games but thats just me 😝

reviews.review_title: Recon 2 Is My New VR Obsession
reviews.review_description:
As a big fan of the first game, I’m really impressed by how the studio has taken things to the next level. Containment Initiative 2: Recon expands on everything that made the original great — deeper modes, larger environments, and more strategic gameplay.

The scavenging mode, in particular, feels much more fleshed out, with longer missions and richer tactical elements. I also love how the studio cleverly reused assets from the first game to create a sense of continuity and familiarity, while still introducing new mechanics and progression systems that make each playthrough rewarding.

Crafting explosives to unlock previously inaccessible areas adds a great sense of discovery, and the steady stream of updates keeps players coming back for more.

Overall, it’s a well-crafted sequel that builds beautifully on its predecessor — I’m personally a huge fan of this game and can’t wait to see where the devs take it next.

Great job on the game Devs! I wasn’t expecting much going in and this game has me addicted for sure. Great game to jump quick and have some fun. 

Positives -
Visuals are solid - especially like gore effects, zombies look good.
Maps are varied and enough detail to give immersion
Gunplay is great - sound, impact, and accuracy 
Looting and upgrades incentive to replay

Ideas -
Need coop/multiplayer 
More maps - gladly pay for expansion 
Larger maps - to accommodate coop and longer 
range combat-maybe hardware constraints 
More story and maybe additional items needed to progress story like batteries and c4 are used currently.
Options for optics such as red dot or holo (maybe scopes)and more customization (gun camos) - more items to unlock and reason to loot
Laser should be brighter using NV
Grenades?
More enemy variety 
More of everything! lol

Love the game and worth every penny and more. Please keep content coming and thanks for the fun experience.
If this game is basically done I don’t think it’s very good. If it’s still being worked on I think these games have crisp shooting.

I enjoyed the game, but didn't realize it was complete. I thought early access meant still be developed. As in more content coming. I'm starting to see it just means it still has bugs and what not. It was fun. A bit short but I still liked it.

developer_response.body: I still plan on adding more unlocks and some other content over the coming months. Thanks for leaving a review!

reviews.review_description: I really enjoyed the first part. The night scenarios and later levels with the larger monsters were particularly oppressive and often made me panic. Part 2 is a definite upgrade. The maps are large, and the new idea of ​​using a truck instead of a helicopter is quite nice. However, the helicopter's time delay often led to a lot of hectic gameplay. Overall, though, it's a great sequel where you're often fighting to the limit. Multiplayer would be perfect!

This game gets crazier than I expected but of course it does. I played the first one so I should have known. The whole idea of filling up your truck with necessary items is fun. The slow motion with headshots thing is pretty cool too but I'm not sure is it supposed to happen every time or just every few? I only ask because I thought it was every time but it doesn't happen every time. Either way I'm enjoying it it's fun and it's only 10 bucks
Please add multiplayer, seated play, and automatic running activity.

Great concept. The ability to run faster needs to be improved. Can’t pickup batteries and some objects to go to next level. The gun won’t go to my left side even though I picked left hand in options. Great sounds and feel with pistol, I have not went far enough for an automatic. The reason I’m not returning this game is in hopes of developers fixing the bugs and improving payability. If those things are fixed this would be a great game. Multiple player would also be good.

developer_response.body:
I can definitely look into these issues and try to get a fix out this week or next. Also, can you specify which items you were having difficulty picking up? Are you sure you were close enough to pick them up? And for the run ability, is your feedback that it should just last longer? That was a change I was considering making anyways. Feel free to reach out here: containmentinitiative@gmail.com

Thanks again for the feedback! I'll definitely get any potential bugs sorted out ASAP!

Edit #1: Just rolled out a hotfix to address the left handed issue. The gun holster will now appear on your left hip once you select the option. Thanks for bringing that to my attention!

reviews.review_description: Can you add more different creatures zombie dogs and can you slow down the speed on the running when you start running it goes out too fast before you can get a chance to even run can you get it to where it lasts a little longer year chance at least to try to get away where it'll go out too fast and sometimes when I crouch to bend over to pick up some off the ground I end up grabbing my knife for a ammo off my belt or something trying to grab a weapon cuz it gets in the way when you cross down your body just gets in the way of you grabbing it unless you hold yourself a certain way and you find a way you can where you can fix that and can you add some kind of flashlight on your helmet to where you can turn on or night vision when you play the dark levels and I'm not sure if the game was to be like that or not but when you inside the sewers I think it's called or wherever it is with tunnels or when the light flickers and flash and cut off the flashlight on your gun cuts off too so I'm not sure if that's a glitch or not cuz once the light shuts off your flashlight sets off for a minute before it pops back on and I'm not sure if it's supposed to do that or not because when it's dark you can't see nothing when you going to dark air with the in some of the guns don't you can't quit flashlights so it kind of useless when you playing games with the levels with the dark areas

Saw some gameplay footage on You Tube and for only $10 decided to give it a try, glad I did! It's a fun zombie horde shooter with some light puzzle elements thrown in. Love the gameplay loop, especially trying to load up your truck with the larger loot boxes and scavenging to have enough to upgrade back at the safehouse. Keeps you going back for more. It'd be nice if you could include a seated option

This is a really solid shooter and a HUGE upgrade from their first game. I really enjoyed the first game but it got old since it was a stationary wave shooter.   This is a full fledged campaign with fun unlocks and objectives. I’m hooked. Well worth the $10.  
The gun physics are second to none

One minor complaint…. You really need to increase the amount of the walking booster… it lasts for sooooo little time… maybe 2-3 seconds and then it’s depleted, which takes 15-20 seconds to fully recharge. Is my avatar a heavy set, 2-pack-a-day smoker? Lol

Update:
The only other complaint I have is the lack of sight upgrades on certain weapons, like the revolver and shotgun. I never wanna use those weapons on difficult missions cuz they don’t have reliable sights. Stinks because I really wanna use the revolver more but there’s no upside really.
Sometimes this is just the game to play when I want to headshot hoards of zombies after a hard day.
Pretty good zombie shooter with plenty of nice weapons and upgrades to unlock as you progress.  I've no idea if it's better than the original.

reviews.review_title: Great game with a high quality level!
reviews.review_description: I grabbed Containment Initiative 2 on the Meta headset and I’m really enjoying it. The gunplay and shooting mechanics feel great. There’s voice acting, a cool backstory, and the whole thing feels surprisingly big-budget for such a small studio. Runs smooth on Quest 2 and honestly feels like a bargain for the quality you get!
Needs some small tweaks and maybe larger levels but solid zombie game.

reviews.review_title: good game but can do better
reviews.review_description: Good game, a seated mod and a Polish graphic and more content for the fair of this game one must have.

I'm enjoying this.  Slight Arizona Sunshine vibes, but rogue like, and cheaper.

It's not as polished as AS, but still fun.
